mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and expanding economic activity, which translates into higher consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically boosts consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is maintained at historically low levels, it signals an accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital for businesses and encouraging investment, which supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low interest rates reduce corporate borrowing costs.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% for at least three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies robust consumer demand and strong purchasing power. This joint strength directly boosts corporate revenues and profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND UNRATE change < 0.1pp MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP.
failure_condition: The interaction effect of positive RSAFS growth and negative UNRATE change does not significantly predict positive SPX 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of expanding industrial activity, consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong business investment and manufacturing output. This indicates a healthy supply side of the economy, boosting corporate earnings and supporting equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.2% MoM for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key indicator of manufacturing health.
failure_condition: In periods identified as industrial expansion, SPX 60-day returns do not significantly outperform periods of industrial stagnation or contraction.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) at or below 5.0% signals a tight labor market, which historically precedes wage inflation and potential central bank tightening. This can compress corporate profit margins and increase discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low unemployment can signal overheating economy.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive or flat in more than 60% of triggered instances when the unemployment rate is at or below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong residential investment and consumer confidence in the housing market and broader economy. This contributes to overall economic growth and positive market sentiment, driving S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading economic indicator.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with SPX 5-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and stable, combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS) growth, it indicates a 'goldilocks' economic scenario: healthy consumer demand without immediate inflationary pressures. This environment allows for sustained economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth < 0.2% MoM AND RSAFS growth > 0.3% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Low inflation and strong demand are ideal for equities.
failure_condition: The combined signal of low CPIAUCSL growth and positive RSAFS growth does not lead to significantly positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, strong annualized GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a healthy and expanding economy. This underpins corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary measure of economic health.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive during periods when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized for two consecutive quarters.
